title: Add perf_span timing to all SSE endpoints + key sync handlers
description: Sprinkle perf_span() context managers across hot paths: /api/tracks (snapshot vs build), all 6 SSE endpoints (per-track inner span + outer total span), and CacheStore.get_* methods (cold vs warm).
acceptanceCriteria: perf_span('tracks.build') wraps the snapshot rebuild path in /api/tracks.
perf_span('tracks.cached') wraps the cached return path.
Each SSE endpoint has perf_span('<endpoint>.compute') around the parallel compute call and perf_span('<endpoint>.write') around the writer commit.
CacheStore.get_* methods have perf_span('cache.<table>.hit') / 'miss' around their dispatch logic.
Overhead when disabled is unmeasurable (<1us per span).
tests/test_perf.py asserts spans accumulate when AUTOCUE_PERF=1.

steps.details: with perf_span('tracks.build'): build_payload(); with perf_span('tracks.cached'): return payload.
_compute_stage outer: with perf_span(f'{endpoint}.compute'): ...; _writer_stage per-track inner: with perf_span(f'{endpoint}.write_one'): commit.
In each get_* method: name = 'cache.energy.hit' if row matches else 'cache.energy.miss'; with perf_span(name): ... .
tests/test_perf.py: enable + drive each endpoint with 10 tracks; query get_stats for each span name; assert count > 0 and durations are reasonable.

technicalNotes: Don't span inside tight inner loops (per-array-element) - reserve for per-track and per-request boundaries.
Names should follow 'subsystem.operation.outcome' for easy aggregation.
